assessor_1_raw: The transformer ticket escalation design is methodologically sound. Temporal split with product-line stratification is appropriate. The equal tuning budget is well-specified. PR-AUC with ECE secondary metric is the right combination for imbalanced classification with calibration concerns. Agent leakage prevention is thorough. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: Solid experiment design. The operational label from workflow logs eliminates annotation issues. 36 months provides sufficient training signal. The temporal split mirrors deployment. Both models receive identical data and equivalent tuning. PR-AUC correctly prioritizes recall-precision tradeoff for rare escalation events. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: Well-designed escalation prediction study. The transformer's sequence modeling with timing information is the right hypothesis to test against a metadata+BoW baseline. Confound controls (product-line stratification, agent leakage prevention, temporal design) are comprehensive. Secondary metrics cover all necessary dimensions. No flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous defense_wins from all three assessors. Experiment design consistently rated as sound.
